description: Concurrency models, parallelism, and synchronization in programming languages.

facts.context: Mutex (mutual exclusion) prevents data races by:
facts.truth: allowing only one thread to access data at a time
distractors: making all data immutable so no writes can occur
automatically parallelizing all operations on the data
copying data for each thread so they never share

facts.truth: waiting circularly for locks held by each other
distractors: executing too fast for the scheduler to manage properly
accessing read-only data without any synchronization
using too much memory causing the system to freeze

facts.context: The actor model (Erlang, Akka) provides concurrency via:
facts.truth: message passing with no shared mutable state
distractors: shared memory protected by fine-grained locking everywhere
global interpreter locks preventing true parallelism
automatic thread creation for every function call

facts.context: CSP (Communicating Sequential Processes) in Go uses:
distractors: shared memory with mutex locks for all data access
callback-based asynchronous event handling only
automatic parallelization without explicit concurrency
multiple OS threads running preemptively in parallel
purely synchronous blocking code with no concurrency
hardware-level parallelism using multiple CPU cores

facts.context: Software Transactional Memory provides atomicity via:
facts.truth: optimistic execution with rollback on conflict
distractors: hardware locks that prevent any concurrent access
copying all memory before every transaction starts
disabling all concurrency during transaction execution

facts.context: Green threads (goroutines, Erlang processes) differ from OS threads by:
facts.truth: being scheduled by runtime, not kernel
distractors: using more memory than OS threads typically require
being slower due to extra runtime overhead
requiring explicit yield calls at every point

facts.context: A data race differs from a race condition in that:
facts.truth: it involves unsynchronized concurrent memory access
distractors: the terms are exact synonyms with identical meaning
race conditions are always bugs while data races are not
data races only occur in single-threaded programs

facts.context: A language memory model (Java, C++) specifies:
facts.truth: what reorderings compilers and hardware may perform
distractors: how much RAM programs can allocate maximum
the layout of objects in memory at runtime
garbage collection algorithm implementation details
are always computed immediately when created
can never fail or throw exceptions ever
must be awaited on the same thread only
